audit_status.source_base: Greek 1 Peter text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact draft reviewed for wives/husbands, inner adornment, good conscience, suffering for good, spirits in prison, baptism.

verses.restored: Likewise wives, be arranged under your own husbands, so that even if some disobey the word, they may be won without word through the conduct of wives,
observing your pure conduct in reverence.
Let your adornment not be outward braiding of hair and wearing of gold or putting on garments,
but the hidden human of the heart, in the incorruptible of gentle and quiet spirit, which is very precious before the Divine.
For so once also the holy women hoping in the Divine adorned themselves, being arranged under their own husbands,
as Sarah obeyed Abraham, calling him lord; whose children you became, doing good and not fearing any terror.
Husbands likewise, living together according to knowledge, giving honor to the feminine as weaker vessel, as also co-heirs of grace of life, so that your prayers may not be hindered.
Finally, all be of one mind, sympathetic, sibling-loving, tenderhearted, humble-minded,
not repaying harm for harm or reviling for reviling, but instead blessing, because into this you were called, so that you may inherit blessing.
For the one willing to love life and see good days, let one stop the tongue from harm and lips from speaking deceit,
let one turn away from harm and do good; let one seek peace and pursue it.
Because eyes of Lord are upon righteous/just ones and his ears toward their prayer, but face of Lord is against those doing harm.
And who is the one harming you if you become zealous for the good?
But even if you should suffer because of righteousness/justice, blessed are you. Do not fear their fear, nor be troubled.
But set apart Messiah as Lord in your hearts, always ready for defense to everyone asking you a word concerning the hope in you, yet with gentleness and reverence,
having a good conscience, so that in what you are spoken against, those reviling your beautiful conduct in Messiah may be put to shame.
For it is better, if the will of Divine should will, to suffer doing good than doing harm.

Because Messiah also suffered once concerning sins/misalignments, righteous/just on behalf of unrighteous/unjust, so that he might bring you to the Divine, put to death in flesh but made alive in Spirit,
in which also he went and proclaimed to the spirits in prison,
who formerly disobeyed, when the patience of the Divine waited in days of Noah, while ark was being prepared, in which few, that is eight souls/lives, were brought safely through water.
Which also now as corresponding pattern saves/heals you: baptism, not putting away dirt of flesh, but appeal/pledge of good conscience toward Divine, through resurrection of Jesus Messiah,
who is at right hand of Divine, having gone into heaven, messengers and authorities and powers being arranged under him.

safety_note: 1 Peter 3:1-7 must not be used to sanctify patriarchy, coercion, or domestic abuse. The passage is held within the wider command to do good without fear, husbands honoring wives as co-heirs of grace of life, and prayers being hindered by dishonor. Reception-history audit required.
translation_notes: Apologia is defense/explanation of hope with gentleness and reverence, not combative apologetics.
Spirits in prison and baptism correspondence require second-pass audit; ambiguity is preserved.
